content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed both amendments: (1) Replaced the fixed 1-second sleep in spawn_background_reap_calls_real_function with a polling loop (50ms intervals, 10s deadline) that checks weak.upgrade().is_none(), making the test robust to CI scheduling jitter while fast in the common case (0.05s). (2) Re-applied git rm --cached result to remove the Nix store symlink from git tracking (the round 5 staging was lost because no commit was made); the deletion is now staged and result remains in .gitignore to prevent re-addition.

## Steps

0. [Completed] Verify result symlink is still tracked (git ls-files result returned 'result') — round 5 staging was lost
1. [Completed] Re-run git rm --cached result to stage the deletion
2. [Completed] Replace fixed 1s sleep in spawn_background_reap_calls_real_function with polling loop: check weak.upgrade().is_none() every 50ms, assert failure if 10s deadline exceeded
3. [Completed] Verify test passes: spawn_background_reap_calls_real_function completes in 0.05s (fast common case, robust to 10s)
4. [Completed] Verify cargo fmt --check passes
5. [Completed] Verify full test suite: 585 unit + 756 integration tests passed, 0 failed (1 pre-existing flaky test passed on rerun)
6. [Completed] Verify result deletion is staged and result is no longer in git ls-files

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test spawn_background_reap_calls: 1 passed in 0.05s (polling loop, no fixed sleep)
- cargo test: 585 unit + 756 integration tests passed, 0 failed
- cargo fmt --check: clean
- git diff --cached --stat: result | 1 - (deletion staged)
- git ls-files result: empty (no longer tracked)
- Pre-existing flaky test checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_includes_workspace_in_checkpoint_commits failed once, passed on rerun — unrelated to this change

## Outstanding Risks

- The result deletion is staged but not yet committed — it will be included in the next commit on this branch
- On platforms where SIGKILL is truly ineffective (uninterruptible I/O 'D' state), the process remains as a zombie after handle drop — inherent to the OS; the timeout correctly avoids blocking the tokio runtime
- Pre-existing clippy warnings (16 in other files) remain unfixed — not introduced by this change
- Pre-existing flaky test checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_includes_workspace_in_checkpoint_commits occasionally fails with 'No such file or directory' — not related to this change
